name: fe4_swap
drl: rule K salience 7 when $p : P() $b : E2(ts == 0) then end
_finding: FLAG-EAGER ARC polarity twin (D-179): K@7 `P() E2(ts==0)` — the corpse on the join node's RIGHT. Oracle 3x []; engine [K]. Trace: the flush folds fresh P(1) into lefts; at the pop the corpse E2(0) walks as a ph=4 pre-link right and the right walk corpse-checks only the left partner [P(1)] — BOTH walk directions carry the blind spot. MECHANISM (D-179, trace-pinned): the plain-join insert walks corpse-check only the PARTNER side (right walk: l.iter().any(is_expired) on left partners, phreak.rs ~1930; left walk: is_expired(f) on right partners, ~1954) — the WALKING fact/tuple is never flag-checked. A corpse whose own memory-fold is deferred past its flagging — unlinked-path staging accumulation (D-101/fz_7_145) or the flush right-stash (D-102) — walks in flagged and forms pairs. The temporal arm does NOT share the blind spot (fe7 green). Fix shape (NOT ported, Bryan gate): corpse-guard the partner LOOP of both plain walks (memory push stays — flag-eager, retraction-lazy); check the AB-arm sites (~595/612, same partner-only pattern, no witness yet) at port time.
